title: MFFC — the "private cone" of a node

steps.title: Two outputs (t, g); n is shared between them
steps.narrative:
An MFFC w.r.t. a cut is the set of internal ANDs that would die if the target were replaced — but only counting nodes above the cut whose only consumer (transitively) is the target.

This AIG has two outputs: t (target) and g. n is consumed by both (look at n's [2]). So even though n is in t's cone, n is not private to t — it must survive any replacement of t. The MFFC walk needs to detect this and reject n.

Cut for t: {p, q, e, h}. p, q are internal ANDs (fanout 1 into the cone); e, h are PIs (fanout 1 into the cone).

entries.n: e · h (fanout [2] — feeds t AND g)

panes.title: Expected MFFC
panes.body: { t, m }. n is rejected because its fanout count won't reach zero. p, q are protected by the boundary boost. e, h, y, g, PO_g are never touched.

steps.title: Counterfactual: WITHOUT the boost
steps.narrative:
deref(t) → deref(m) — m's fanout is 1, recurses fully. deref(p) → a, b are PIs; p added wrongly. deref(q) → c, d; q added wrongly.
Then deref(n): 2 → 1, NOT zero — n is correctly excluded (sharing detection works WITHOUT the boost; sharing is a stronger protection than boost). But p, q are wrongly absorbed.

Result: count = 4; MFFC = {t, m, p, q}. n correctly out, but p, q wrongly in.

entries.n (already OK): rejected via fanout-2 sharing — boost not needed for n

steps.title: Apply boundary boost: +1 on every cut leaf
steps.narrative: abcRewrite.c:138..148 brackets the deref with a +1/-1 on each cut-leaf's fanout count. Boost only matters for cut leaves with fanout 1; for cut leaves that already have multiple consumers it's redundant (but harmless).

steps.title: Deref t → count = 1, mark t
steps.narrative: deref(t): count = 1, label t with current TravId. Walk into fanins m and n.

steps.title: Boost saves p (m.fanin0): 2 → 1, NOT zero — STOP
steps.narrative:
p is a cut leaf. Boosted count: 2. Decrement → 1. Not zero → recursion STOPS. p is not added to the MFFC.

Without the boost, p would have gone 1 → 0 here and we'd have walked into a, b — the wrong outcome.

steps.title: Same for q (m.fanin1): 2 → 1, STOP
steps.narrative: q is the other cut leaf reached from m. Same outcome.

steps.title: Sharing rejects n: 2 → 1, NOT zero — n NOT in MFFC
steps.narrative:
Back at t, deref the second fanin n. n's fanout count is 2 (it feeds t AND g). Decrement → 1. Not zero → recursion STOPS, n is not added to the MFFC.

This is a different rejection mechanism from the boost: n is excluded because it has a real external consumer (g). The deref leaves n alive, exactly as it should — any replacement of t will reuse n's output via the existing edge.

Note: e, h were never decremented — the recursion didn't enter n.

panes.body: Boost vs. sharing — both stop deref recursion. Boost is artificial protection ABC adds for cut leaves; sharing is real Boolean-network protection that exists naturally. Both yield the same effect: count doesn't reach zero, no recursion, node not in MFFC.

steps.title: Result: MFFC = {t, m} — 2 of 6 internal ANDs
steps.narrative:
Recursion done. count = 2. The 2 red nodes are exactly the ANDs private to t. The 4 cut leaves (blue) survive via boost. n survives via sharing — its fanout to g protected it without any artificial boost. g, y, PO_g are entirely untouched.

The MFFC is 2 out of 6 ANDs, not the whole AIG. That's the point — it's a strict subset, distinguishing "owned by t" from "in t's cone".

entries.Cut leaves (blue): p, q, e, h — via boost
entries.Untouched (gray): y, g, PO_g, plus a, b, c, d below the cut

panes.title: Re-reference pass
panes.body: Abc_NodeMffcLabelAig follows up with a re-ref pass walking the same red path in reverse, restoring every fanout count. Then the +1 boost on cut leaves is stripped. The AIG is byte-identical to its original state — only the red nodes carry the current TravId.
